description: MG996R metal-gear servo — parametric functional EOL test (commanded move + measured response).

functional.comment: Limits are per-measurement spec windows. The instrument reports raw readings; the host checks them against these. UNCALIBRATED PLACEHOLDER VALUES -- this unit is an extended-travel MG996R (~180 degrees each way from center, so close to a full turn end to end), not the common ~120-degree hobby servo, so range_deg is set to a wide provisional band rather than a guessed exact figure. Run 20-30 known-good cycles once the fixture is built (see docs/RUNBOOK.md, calibration section) and narrow every window to what you actually measure before trusting a verdict.

speed_dps.comment: range_deg / settle_ms, i.e. average deg/s over the fixed settle window, not a peak/instantaneous rate -- it under-reports true speed by however much settle_ms exceeds actual sweep time. Keep settle_ms tight to the real sweep time for a meaningful reading.

station.comment: Single-board ESP32-CAM (as MCU): functional + HMI over one USB serial (mcu_port). Servo PWM on GPIO13; I2C (SDA=14, SCL=15) shared by OLED 0x3c, AS5600 0x36, INA219 0x40.
